title: Filipino Lumpia (Fried Spring Rolls)

subs.name: 300 g finely diced mixed vegetables (cabbage, carrot, mushroom) for a vegetarian lumpia
subs.notes: sauté the vegetable filling first to remove moisture before wrapping

subs.name: 1.5 tbsp Maggi masala sauce for an Indian-pantry adaptation
2 tbsp maida paste (flour + water) for sealing if avoiding egg in the wrapper
subs.notes: a flour-water slurry seals just as effectively

ingredients.name: maida (for the spring roll wrappers or as a paste to seal)
visual_checks: use to make homemade wrappers (thin crepe style) or buy spring roll pastry if available
ingredients.notes: use to make homemade wrappers (thin crepe style) or buy spring roll pastry if available

subs.name: ready-made spring roll pastry sheets (available in frozen section at large supermarkets)

ingredients.name: cornflour (for homemade wrappers)
visual_checks: add to maida for crispier homemade wrappers
ingredients.notes: add to maida for crispier homemade wrappers

ingredients.name: green chilli sauce or sweet chilli sauce (for dipping)

steps.text: If making homemade wrappers: whisk maida and cornflour with a pinch of salt and enough water (about 200 ml) to make a thin, pourable batter. Heat a lightly oiled flat pan on medium; pour 3 tbsp batter and swirl into a thin 18 cm crepe. Cook 1 minute until the top is dry. Stack with parchment between them. You need 12 wrappers.

Mix the minced meat, grated carrot, spring onion, garlic, onion, soy sauce, pepper and half the beaten egg until evenly combined. Season with salt — the soy sauce already adds salt, so taste as you go.

Lay a wrapper on a clean surface. Place 2 tbsp of filling in a thin line about 3 cm from the bottom edge. Roll upward tightly, fold in the sides, and continue rolling. Seal the edge with the remaining beaten egg. Repeat with all wrappers.

Heat 2 cups oil in a kadai or deep frying pan to 175°C (a small piece of wrapper should bubble vigorously within 3 seconds). Fry the lumpia in batches of 4–5, turning, for 4–5 minutes until deep golden and the filling is cooked through.
Drain on a wire rack or paper. Do not stack while hot or the wrappers lose their crunch. Serve immediately with sweet chilli sauce or a quick dip of vinegar, garlic and pinch of sugar.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: keema → 300 g finely diced mixed vegetables (cabbage, carrot, mushroom) for a vegetarian lumpia (sauté the vegetable filling first to remove moisture before wrapping); maida → ready-made spring roll pastry sheets (available in frozen section at large supermarkets) (much quicker and crispier result); soy-sauce → 1.5 tbsp Maggi masala sauce for an Indian-pantry adaptation; egg → 2 tbsp maida paste (flour + water) for sealing if avoiding egg in the wrapper (a flour-water slurry seals just as effectively)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Lumpia maps closely to Indian spring rolls. Make the wrapper dough exactly as you would for a thin dosa batter-style crepe using maida. Minced chicken (kothu kozhi) or mince from any Indian butcher works perfectly. Deep fry in a kadai with neutral oil. Chilli garlic sauce from any Indian store works as the dipping sauce. Ready-made samosa pastry sheets can replace spring roll wrappers in a pinch.
